features.name: Zero Server Management
features.description: Completely abstracts physical servers, VMs, and OS patching, allowing developers to focus solely on code.

features.name: Instant Autoscaling
features.description: Scales compute instances up instantly to handle heavy incoming traffic spikes, and down to zero when idle.

features.name: Event-Driven Invocations
features.description: Triggers code execution automatically in response to events from Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, or HTTP requests.

iamRoles.description: Full administrative control over Cloud Run services, configurations, and revisions.
Full access to create, update, delete, and configure Cloud Functions.

cliExamples.description: Deploy a containerized application to Cloud Run
Deploy an event-driven Cloud Function triggered by a Pub/Sub topic

design: Serverless compute platforms are selected for stateless, variable, or event-driven applications.
Cloud Run runs stateless containers, supporting any binary, framework, or language.
Cloud Run functions (formerly Cloud Functions) deploy small snippets of code for simple, isolated tasks.
App Engine Standard is a PaaS for standard languages, offering fast scaling and pre-configured runtimes.
App Engine Flexible runs custom Docker containers on managed Compute Engine VMs, scaling slower than Standard.
Cloud Run supports up to 250 concurrent requests per container instance, reducing cold start rates.
Serverless VPC Access connectors are required to access resources (like Cloud SQL, Memorystore) with private IPs.
Applications must be stateless; write local scratch data only to the ephemeral /tmp directory.
security: Manage public service availability using IAM roles (allUsers for public, specific users/accounts for private).
Assign a custom Service Account to your serverless service to enforce least privilege to other GCP APIs.
Integrate Cloud Run/App Engine with Cloud Armor by routing traffic through an external HTTP(S) Load Balancer.
Mount secrets from Secret Manager directly as environment variables or volume mounts instead of hardcoding.
Configure Ingress settings to 'Internal' to prevent external traffic from accessing internal microservices.
Restrict serverless functions from accessing other projects using project-specific VPC Service Controls.
Verify container image security using Artifact Analysis before deploying them to Cloud Run.
Enable Binary Authorization to block deployment of unsigned, untrusted container images.
reliability: Serverless runtimes automatically replicate application containers across multiple zones in the region.
Set the minimum instances configuration to '1' or more to eliminate cold-start latency for key endpoints.
Autoscaling limits (maximum instances) prevent runaway scaling and protect downstream databases from crashing.
Configure HTTP health checks to verify that container instances are ready before routing traffic to them.
Canary deployments are supported out of the box using traffic splitting between revision states.
Logs written to stdout/stderr are captured automatically and exported directly to Cloud Logging.
Event-driven functions process events asynchronously, supporting automatic retries on execution failures.
Design services to handle duplicate events gracefully, maintaining idempotency.
cost: Serverless platforms bill to the nearest 100ms based on CPU and memory allocated during active request execution.
Idle resources cost zero because instances auto-scale down to zero when request traffic stops.
Choose CPU allocation mode: 'CPU allocated only during request processing' (saves cost) vs 'CPU always allocated'.
Set max instances to cap maximum possible spending and protect against DDoS billing spikes.
Optimize container startup times (e.g. lightweight base images) to reduce billing during scaling cold starts.
Tune container request concurrency limits to maximize resource efficiency per active instance.
Use Cloud Billing budgets and Pub/Sub notifications to automate service shutdown if cost thresholds are crossed.
Take advantage of the generous free tiers offered monthly for Cloud Run and Cloud Run functions.
